title: Silky Herbal Algerian Chicken Tagine
description: A fragrant, lowfat chicken tagine with a silky sauce, infused with ground cinnamon and fresh herbs, cooked sousvide for a tender, crowdpleasing bite.
instructions: Season the chicken breasts with salt, pepper, ground cinnamon, cumin, coriander, paprika, and ginger.
Lay the sliced onion and minced garlic in the bottom of a vacuum‑seal bag, then place the seasoned chicken on top.
Add the lemon juice, a splash of chicken broth, and drizzle with olive oil. Seal the bag using a vacuum sealer.
Submerge the sealed bag into the preheated water bath and cook for 90 minutes.
After the sous‑vide, remove the bag and pat the chicken dry with paper towels. Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium‑high heat and sear the chicken for 2–3 minutes per side until golden.
Transfer the seared chicken to a shallow pan, pour a little of the cooking liquid left in the bag, and simmer gently for 5 minutes to develop sauce.
Stir in chopped cilantro and parsley, adjust seasoning with extra salt or pepper if needed.
Serve the silky chicken and sauce over a bed of couscous, rice, or flatbread if desired.
Garnish with extra fresh herbs and a squeeze of lemon.
Enjoy a fragrant, low‑fat tagine that’s perfect for crowd‑pleasing gatherings.
